inline_comments.message: rename to anchor

this is not a good exception choice, it's an extension of CpsException
it means on a REST level it will be converted into internal server error. 
see https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS+Exceptions+and+REST+API+HTTP+Response+Codes
suggested the new exception AnchorNotFoundException (extends CpsAdminException)

a bit more then 30 seconds but OK and I see Rishi has done that now. In general I would advise that we do create separate tasks and separate commits for such unrelated activities. SO hopefully we wont need such discussion in future :-)

this method will only fail in case the CpsAdminPersistenceService.getAnchor()

as agreed, just add a quick groovy test to cover the constructor in this module.
It need to verify the resulting string in the exception

just for future reference, all scenario descriptions (title of the tests) should end in a full stop '.' as per our conventions. (I forgot about this myself and there are many legacy test that don't have this but its really not important and will fix as we go)

the descriptions for labels like given: then: do NOT start with capital or have full stop. The reason for this is that the label itself is already part of the 'sentence' as we cannot control its casing...
Expected value should be the first argument.
Not null annotations from the interface are missing.

We can choose to create another common method with the common code LN 112 to 115. Refer below comment as well.
dataspaceRepository.getByName(dataspaceName) invoked twice in this method, one in getAnchor() and other in getYangSchemaResources().
agreed
its is 2 calls, I can live with it...
it will be solved by cache on  top, so this should be that much consumed
shouldn't
see comment below.
this was discussed and since we will have cache that DB call will be cached.

Its good to have DB cache, but I still think when there is an option to reduce the duplicate DB calls, that should be done.
Get APIs can often be hit by performance issues.

some short time ago I was pointed that this test is only for cover persistence. Not sure if that has changed.
I'm ok with the test here

I thought you originally were taling about code duplication.

I think some refactoring is required to have one common method to get the resources and build a SchemaContext which can later be cached (I already suggested that in one of the first iterations)

the acceptance criteria for the Cache userstory clearly state that the additional call should be prevented, see https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-163

But this is not in the scope of this user story

not sure what is meant here, anyway I am OK with this tets too

(at some stage I would like to see if we can convert these tets to make them more readable so we see more clearly what behavior is being testes and what exactly is asserted)
